content.full_text:
LAW OFFICES
TODD AND SIEGEL
A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION

JOHN SANFORD TODD
SAMUEL E. SIEGEL
SCOTT E. NICHOLS

4909 LAKEWOOD BOULEVARD, SUITE 300
LAKEWOOD, CALIFORNIA 90712-2405

TELEPHONES
(213) 531-1805
(213) 634-0404
TELECOPIER
(213) 634-0405

October 31, 1990

PLEASE REPLY TO:
OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY
4909 Lakewood Boulevard, Suite 300
Lakewood, California 90712
(231) 634-1217

California Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Attention: Blanca Breeze

Re: Potential Conflicts of Interest for City of La Puente City
    Councilman and Planning Commissioner

Dear Ms. Breeze:

The City Attorney of the City of La Puente is requesting written
advice pursuant to Government Code Section 83114(b) concerning
potential conflicts of interest for a City Councilman and a
Planning Commissioner in the City of La Puente.

The City of La Puente is currently in the process of adopting a
Redevelopment Plan. The members of the La Puente City Council also
serve as the La Puente Redevelopment Agency. During the course of
this procedure, a City Councilman and a Planning Commissioner have
advised our office of factual situations which may give rise to
potential conflicts of interest pursuant to Government Code Section
87100-87103. Therefore, the City Attorney is requesting a written
opinion from the Fair Political Practices Commission as to whether
said Councilman and Planning Commissioner would have a conflict of
interest and be disqualified from participating in matters
concerning procedures relating to the La Puente Redevelopment Plan
adoption based on the following factual situations.

City Councilman Louis R. Perez' wife works parttime in a small
retail women's dress store located within the Redevelopment Project
Area, and is a salaried employee. Her earnings were in excess of
$250.00 for the past year.

That appears to be the only potential conflict of interest on the
part of Councilman Perez.

Fair Political Practices Commission
October 31, 1990
Page 2

Planning Commissioner Arthur Gutierrez owns his place of residence
which is within 300 feet of the boundary of a redevelopment project
area. The property is currently zoned R-1 and is general planned
for multiple family residential high density. The size of the lot
is 11,160 square feet. The highest and best use of the property
would be for the construction of three rental units although Mr.
Gutierrez currently has no plans other than to reside in the
residence. There is currently one single family residence located
on the lot.

Within the City of La Puente, residential lots equal to or
exceeding that of Mr. Gutierrez constitute approximately 26% of the
total residential lots in the City including schools and 12% of
residential lots in the City not including schools.

The Project Area which is within 300 feet of the property owned by
Mr. Gutierrez is for the upgrading of an old downtown area. The
property is all zoned Downtown Business District and does not
directly affect the zoning or use of the property owned by Mr.
Gutierrez.

Based on the above facts, would you please advise this office
whether Councilman Perez or Gutierrez have a conflict of
interest which would disqualify them from participation in
matters relating to the adoption of a Redevelopment Plan.

Your prompt written reply to our request for advice will be
sincerely appreciated. If you need any further information, please
do not hesitate to contact me immediately. Thank you.

Sincerely,

SAMUEL SIEGEL, CITY ATTORNEY
SS:bh

sections.question: Based on the above facts, would you please advise this office whether Councilman Perez or Commissioner Gutierrez have a conflict of interest which would disqualify them from participating in matters relating to the adoption of a Redevelopment Plan.
sections.question_synthetic: Do a City Councilman whose wife works part-time in a retail store within a redevelopment project area, and a Planning Commissioner who owns residential property within 300 feet of a redevelopment project area, have conflicts of interest that would disqualify them from participating in the adoption of a Redevelopment Plan?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a City Attorney to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No conclusion/advice section is present. The question concerns potential conflicts of interest under Government Code Sections 87100-87103. Document dated October 31, 1990.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Based on the above facts, would you please advise this office whether Councilman Perez or Commissioner Gutierrez have a conflict of interest which would disqualify them from participating in matters relating to the adoption of a Redevelopment Plan.

embedding.summary: The City Attorney of La Puente requests written FPPC advice regarding potential conflicts of interest for Councilman Louis Perez (whose wife works in a retail store within the redevelopment project area) and Planning Commissioner Arthur Gutierrez (who owns residential property within 300 feet of the project area) in connection with the adoption of a Redevelopment Plan.
